stimulus.content:
Read the scenario below:

Congress passed a new law about free speech. A group of citizens said the law took away their rights. The case went to the Supreme Court. The Court ruled that the law went against the Constitution and could not be enforced.

stem: How did the Supreme Court use checks and balances in this scenario?

choices.text: It wrote a new law to replace the old one
It struck down a law that broke the Constitution
It asked the President to veto the law
It held an election to let citizens decide

answer_explanation: The Supreme Court checked Congress by ruling the law unconstitutional. Courts do not write laws, ask for vetoes, or hold elections. Judicial review is one of the key checks in the system.
